title: Amsterdam Noord
standfirst: Five minutes across the IJ from Centraal Station — Amsterdam's creative quarter in a former shipyard. Street art the size of buildings, rooftop observation decks, and a beach bar on the waterfront.
intro_html:
Amsterdam Noord was, for most of its history, the city Amsterdam pretended didn't exist. The IJ waterway kept it separate from the historic centre — no bridges, no trams, just the free ferry that workers took to the shipyards every morning. The NDSM wharf (Nederlandsche Dok en Scheepsbouw Maatschappij) employed 10,000 people at its peak. When the last shipyard closed in 1984, it took most of the neighbourhood's economy with it.

Then the artists arrived. In the 1990s, squatters took over the abandoned NDSM buildings. The city, unable to evict them, eventually formalised the arrangement. Studios, cultural venues, and creative businesses followed. Today the NDSM wharf is Amsterdam's most concentrated creative district — home to Greenpeace's international headquarters, MTV Netherlands, and some of the most significant street art in Europe.

Noord is still in transition. The free ferry from Centraal Station takes five minutes and delivers you to a neighbourhood that feels genuinely different from the tourist-dense historic centre. The views back across the IJ — the water, the station, the medieval city behind it — are some of the best in Amsterdam. Come for a half-day. Stay for a full one.

todos.title: A'DAM Lookout
todos.description:
Standing tall on the banks of the IJ, the A'DAM Lookout is a 360° observation deck that offers the most breathtaking elevated views of Amsterdam. From the 20th floor, you can see the entire historic center, the sprawling port, and the polders that stretch toward the horizon.

For the truly adventurous, the 'Over the Edge' swing allows you to dangle 100 meters above the ground, swinging back and forth over the building's edge. It is a high-octane way to see the city, but even without the swing, the rooftop bar and interactive exhibitions make this a premium North Amsterdam experience.

todos.title: STRAAT Museum
todos.description:
Housed in a massive, 8,000-square-meter former shipyard building on the NDSM wharf, STRAAT is the world's largest museum dedicated to street art and graffiti. The museum features over 150 commissioned murals by international artists, some of which are several stories high, creating an overwhelming gallery of urban creativity.

The industrial setting perfectly complements the raw, expressive energy of the art on display. Walking through the vast, cold hall filled with vibrant, larger-than-life paintings is a transformative experience that challenges the traditional concept of a 'museum' and celebrates the global reach of street culture.

todos.title: Eye Filmmuseum
todos.description:
The Eye Filmmuseum is an architectural marvel—a sleek, white, angular building that resembles a futuristic ship anchored on the IJ waterfront. Inside, it serves as the Netherlands' national center for cinematography, featuring four cinemas, a massive film library, and a series of high-tech exhibitions on the history of film.

The building is free to enter, and its soaring glass-walled cafe offers some of the best waterfront views in the city. Whether you're here to see a rare cult classic or simply to admire the cutting-edge design, the Eye is the undisputed cultural crown of Amsterdam Noord.

todos.title: De Ceuvel
todos.description:
De Ceuvel is a masterclass in sustainable urban regeneration. Built on a formerly contaminated industrial plot, this creative hub consists of old houseboats that have been hoisted onto land and connected by a winding wooden jetty. The entire site is a living laboratory for green technologies, featuring solar power and specialized plants that clean the soil.

Its solar-powered cafe is one of the most atmospheric spots in Noord, offering a seasonal, plant-based menu and house-brewed ginger beer. Dangle your feet over the water from the wooden boardwalks and soak in the 'off-grid' vibe of this unique social enterprise.

foods.title: Hotel de Goudfazant
foods.description:
Despite the name, Hotel de Goudfazant is not a hotel, but a massive, 1,200-square-meter restaurant located in a former garage. The interior is a symphony of industrial chic, featuring vintage cars, high-end chandeliers, and a massive open kitchen that serves exceptional French-inspired cuisine.

The atmosphere is effortlessly cool and spacious, making it a favorite for large groups and art-world insiders. The menu is simple and focused, emphasizing high-quality ingredients and classic techniques in a setting that feels like a secret, high-end warehouse party.

foods.title: Corner Store
foods.description:
Corner Store is the epitome of modern Noord cool. This sleek, minimalist space functions as both a high-fidelity vinyl bar and an avant-garde restaurant, where an impressive sound system provides the backdrop for a menu inspired by Japanese and Southeast Asian flavors.

The focus here is on precision—from the expertly curated record collection to the selection of rare natural wines. It is a sensory playground for those who appreciate the intersection of great music, thoughtful architecture, and experimental cooking.

foods.title: Oedipus Brewing
foods.description:
Oedipus Brewing is one of the most experimental and colorful craft breweries in the Netherlands. Their taproom in Noord is a vibrant, mural-covered space where you can sample their legendary 'Mannenliefde' lemongrass saison or any of their rotating seasonal releases.

The brewery's philosophy is rooted in creativity and community, often hosting live music, art shows, and food pop-ups. It is the perfect place to experience the colorful, alternative spirit of the Noord district while enjoying some of the most innovative beers being produced in the country today.

getting_there.text: Free GVB ferries run 24/7 from behind Centraal Station. Take the Buiksloterweg ferry for the Eye and A'DAM Lookout, or the NDSM ferry for the creative wharf.
Cycling is the best way to see Noord; you can bring your bike on the ferry for free.
